mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a tightening labor market, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production signals strong aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns when both GDPC1 and INDPRO show positive growth in the same period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and elevated inflation expectations compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and consumer spending indicate improving demand conditions, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when both HOUST and RSAFS show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening supply-side capacity, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral earnings disappointments.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates robust labor market conditions, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased consumer spending and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS grows by more than 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with stable inflation indicates a healthy economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns when both GDPC1 grows by more than 2% and CPIAUCSL remains below 2%.
